Friendly understanding management, that works with standers and professional ethics also place interest of their stakeholders at heart,I got two heater that was brought to my room am going to use one and return one,
my only challenge here is that some of tenant forgot the sign to maintain and keep the property clean after used.

Avoid this place like the plague (continuation)
So we were there for 2 weeks without heating and we asked if they would at least give us some heaters temporarily. They agreed on the phone but nobody showed up or supplied us with anything. That house in the winter got cold enough to see our breath inside. I don’t have an exact temp but it was cold.
On the inverse of this however, the windows don’t open due to the safety measures in place. In the summer heat wave, I hope you bring air conditioning or a fan because you will pass out in your hallways. Dangerous levels of heat in that building. Often hotter than the 35/40 degree weather outside.
The internet drops out inconsistently and is great for watching youtube or tv. However it takes ages to download or upload anything of moderate size, be it video games, university work or large files. And the internet inconsistently drops out so frequently that it is impossible to do any live events or games that require the internet. Sometimes even messaging apps such as discord, Skype, teams drop out mid call.
There is no parking allowed on premises so kiss your car goodbye or risk parking it on the street with the crack heads and drug dealers that walk outside the gate.

This one is less to do with the staff and more with the other tenants but the walls are pretty thin, so expect to hear conversations, loud music, washing machines or loud tv’s through walls. And if you have your window open, expect the unpleasant smog of weed stank and the sound of singing to permeate your own living space.

There have also been some occasions in which the staff, (student cribs and 3rd party companies employed by student cribs) have on multiple occasion entered the flat without notice. It has been a huge invasion of privacy and an unpleasant one at that. They are meant to issue you 24 hrs via email or text before they complete a house viewing or maintenance regarding your building and have failed to do so time and time again. Getting as far as entering our rooms while we slept to give a house tour to random people. This is not only illegal but fairly worrying.
On top of this during viewings multiple items had been misplaced or stolen from our rooms, and not only us. Somebody had taken shoe laces, keyboard caps, an entire Nintendo switch just to name a few. Some other tenants from other parts of the block had noticed the same, none of it compensated or replaced by the company.

The fire alarms would be set off multiple times from people smoking, vaping or cooking in their house. It’s sets off the whole complex and we aren’t able to silence it ourselves. Neither can the fire department. It rarely gets silenced within the hour it first goes off so expect to stand outside in the cold for hours at a time in your pyjamas waiting for a lazy staff member to drive down too quiet the building so you can resume your work/sleep. Honestly we have had times where we would rather walk to Wetherspoons for a couple hours to have a meal and drinks, come home and the alarm is still blaring.

Honestly the only good I can think about this place was the location as its close to town and the university so typically you can walk just about anywhere you need fairly quickly.

Don’t risk it. It may be more pricey elsewhere but I will never recommend this place or business to anyone.

AVOID THIS PLACE LIKE THE PLAGUE!!!
I have lived here for the past year and my experience has been horrific.
The Staff are slow to act and unhelpful. The online support is borderline non existent. They leave the building in poor shape, messy and sometimes ignore your problems completely. The advertised “fully furnished” rooms are a lie. There’s so many issues.

From the moment we moved in we were lacking basic equipment and rooms that were promised to us. Our move in was pushed back 5 weeks due to them “renovating the room in which we were to be staying”. (For background context there was 5 of us in the flat, and only bathroom so they said that they were going to install a new one). Once we got there, they had not only somehow not installed a bathroom, but the kitchen was the only thing that had been renovated in the first place and poorly at that. The curtains weren’t hung, all of the bedrooms had old tattered stained carpets. The doors were worn and old. The furniture we did have was coated in paint from the poor job of the staff after painting the walls. We had one working fridge among the 5 of us. Even in the kitchen they miss cut the flooring and ended up using a nail gun to staple the laminate flooring down instead of cutting it to size.

We were missing so many supplies and even after asking for an inventory list or replacement gear for months, we were left with empty promises and had to buy our own. We were missing a dustpan and brush, multiple rubbish bins, a kettle, mop and bucket, sound bar (which should come included with the TV), Toilet brush, Multiple mirrors, entire dressers, a secondary fridge etc, etc. Some of the staff even commented when viewing the suite and said that we were missing the equipment listed but they didn’t do anything about it.

Onto the staff when dealing with issues. They tend to be slow to complete jobs no matter how urgent or how long in advance you remind them of the issues at hand. You can message the staff through 3 main ways, face to face, through the phone, or via email/the student portal. All as useless as the last.
We have had issues with the washer/dryer breaking mid cycle and leaving our clothes stuck within the washer for literal weeks before they helped us remove our gear. Our fire doors were deemed not fire safe at the start of the year, by the time we were moving out they still hadn’t changed them. We’ve had multiple issues of rat infestations due to the lack of bin collection and nothing was done. Towards the end of our stay we even had multiple homeless people enter through the locked gate somehow and they ended up smoking meth on the premises and pissing on the walls, continuing to throw garbage on the floors from out of the bins. The police were called on 2 occasions.
Security is non existent. No cameras, no head office, no security staff. This also means that you have to sit at the front gate if you want any chance of collecting a parcel unless you send it to a local pick up point at a post office.
Furthermore, they have a 24hr emergency call operator for out of hours service that are meant to help with emergencies. They don’t work on site or within Leicester's area, so everything that they do has to be emailed in and you are once again stuck waiting for days. One of my housemates locked himself out of his room and couldn’t remember the combination. Luckily due to a few YouTube tutorials we we’re able to get it open ourselves. However we had reported the issue straight away and we’re left with nobody even acknowledging the issue that they had been locked outside. For over a week we waited just to see if anything would be done, but no. Nothing. My flat mate had we not been able to get into his room would have been left to sleep on the couch or in one of our rooms for over a week.

During the winter period, our heating throughout the entire flat didn’t work. The thermostat was broken when we moved in and nothing was done about it. We were promised that it would be sorted within the week. Nothing. Then they said next week. Nothing.

reviews.text: great place to live, nice homy feel.

Awful experience. At the start of our tenancy all windows had leaks, this took months to resolve. We have also had a leak under our sink, no hot water coming out the shower, fire alarms that go off for hours at a time really late at night and early in the morning, missing furniture all year, a broken freezer, a broken washing machine, the outside bins not being emptied for months. All which took far too long to be resolved. To top this all off there have been rats in the courtyard for weeks, which you can hear squeaking at night. And now we have mice in the flat, it took them three days to have someone come out to start to resolve this, this left me and my flatmates without somewhere to live for three days. Would not recommend. It’s a shame really as it has potential to be a nice flat.

Living at the Shoe and Boot Factory with Student Cribs was hands down one of the worst experiences I’ve ever had. From start to finish, the entire stay was filled with stress, neglect, and a complete lack of professionalism.

Someone broke into our flat, and even after reporting it, nothing was done. There was no compensation, no lock replacement, and absolutely no regard for our safety. We felt completely vulnerable living there. To make matters worse, there was no security on-site at any point, which only made the situation more frightening.

Maintenance was a nightmare. Repairs took forever—our fridge took over a month to be fixed, the dishwasher never worked the entire time we were there (over two years), and flooding in the flat was handled slowly and poorly. There were also rats and bed bugs in the property, which made it feel completely unsafe and unhygienic.

One of the worst parts was the mould growing in the bathroom. It was left untreated for so long that several of us got sick, and it genuinely affected our health. This was not just inconvenient—it was dangerous, and the lack of urgency from management was appalling.

The fire alarm went off every single day for a while, adding even more chaos and stress. And when we tried to raise any of these issues with management, we were met with rude, dismissive, and condescending responses. Communication was awful, and we never felt listened to.

This accommodation is absolutely not worth the money. If I could give it zero stars, I would. Student Cribs needs to seriously sort out how they run their properties. I would never recommend staying here to anyone — not even for a short stay. Stay away.
Harry and Dave were incredibly friendly and approachable. They addressed and resolved the issues in our flat immediately.

Living in the Shoe and Boot Factory with Student Cribs was a terrible experience. The service was awful and maintenance was always slow. Our fridge broke and it took them over a month to fix it. The dishwasher was broken for two years and never got fixed. We had floods in our flat which were eventually dealt with, but not quickly and not properly.

There were rats and bed bugs in the property, which made it feel completely unsafe. We also had mould growing in the bathroom for ages before anything was done about it. Communication was poor and we never felt listened to. Even when things were eventually fixed, it was always too late and never good enough.

Not worth the money at all. Zero out of ten experience. Would not recommend.

I had a fantastic experience staying at The Shoe & Boot Factory student accommodation. The rooms are modern, well-furnished, and equipped with great amenities, including a personal TV in every room, which adds to comfort and convenience.

The location is excellent, with easy access to the university, shops, and transport links. The staff are friendly, approachable, and always ready to assist, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable stay.

Overall, The Shoe & Boot Factory is a great place to live as a student, and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for high-quality student accommodation.

reviews.text: If 0 stars was an option I would definitely give them that. Worst accommodation I’ve ever stayed at. Someone was living in my room over summer seemed like a contractor or builder and it took them all the way till the start of university which was 3rd October to clean my room and I still had to pay my first rent in full. I was also told that everything would be brand new and I had massive stains in my carpet. Took them up until the end of October to sort it out. A piece of the wall randomly fell off in the kitchen which could have hurt someone and it was reported to maintenance which was ignored so we had a hole in the wall with the pipes exposed it’s only when maintenance came to our flat to fix the broken door handle a month later that they saw it and put it back. Don’t stay here don’t let the £99 a week persuade you because you will want to leave when you want to they won’t allow you to unless you find someone to take over your tenancy. Other people have leaks in their flat and they basically say that they take no responsibility for the damage of your goods from robberies or leaks.

reviews.text: This has been the worst accommodation and experience in the 3yrs I've been studying here. I've had so many problems & reported so many faults and the staff are unhelpful and don't really care about my or my flatmates welfare. There has been a leak coming through the light in the kitchen for a week now and it started to smoke we called the fire brigade and they were shocked that the flat was signed off as habitable and concerned about our health and safety a week on and the leak has now travelled to our TV in the dining room and no one has come to even look at it let alone fix it. Other faults reported include the heating which doesn't work, a toilet that doesn't flush, broken kitchen drawers, no cupboards in my bedroom, old carpet when I was promised brand new carpet, no kettle, toaster or microwave to name just a few faults. I was not greeted by anyone on the day I moved in which meant I was under house arrest and could not leave the flat, I wasn't provided with an accurate code to access my bedroom, I'm really dissapointed that a large company treats students this poorly charge full rent, yet take no ownership of any of the issues raised. DON'T STAY HERE THE PLACE IS NOT HABITABLE AND THERE ARE SERIOUS CONCERNS OVER HEALTH AND SAFETY MEASURES. If 0 stars was an option I would have selected it.

The new student cribs staff are nice and friendly. However the building itself has so many faults for the past 3 months we had to regularly shower in cold water. Constant viewings which is really frustrating especially during exam season. And poor wifi.

reviews.text: The fire alarm keeps going off in the middle of the night every now and then. There is no way to stop it except to call the emergency number. You also have to pay extra credit on your phone to call the emergency number (which is a different issue in itself)  and wait for someone to respond. The management has some really good people, who make the experience much better. However, some are just rude and not cooperative at all. The fire alarm once or twice can be seen as a mistake by another tenant. However when it happens at 4 am on a Sunday morning for the third time that week, this to me is outrageous. What’s worse is when you call them, they Do not have a hint of guilt or apology. I called them to complaint and I didn’t even hear the man say sorry for the inconvenience once. Ridiculous. I wouldn’t stay here again if I had a choice. Don’t be swayed by quirky interior this building has, it won’t matter when you can’t sleep in your own flat.

reviews.text: The location is absolutely perfect. Groceries and all other stores and cafes are near. University’s business school is a 15 min walk which in my opinion would be the same if you lived on campus accommodations near st. David’s and cardiac hill (the walk from isca isn’t hilly). It’s nice compared to other accommodation. However, there’s less storage in kitchen and bedrooms. The walls aren’t that sound proof. The building is a bit old so it keeps having its issues now and then, hopefully they fix this when they refurbish this. Maintenance comes at a reasonable time if there’s a problem. Communal area has not that many things, there’s a pool and table tennis and a lounge area with study space which I believe contradicts the purpose of study area. Could’ve been more for the price. Overall, It’s a decent place to stay. Just a bit overpriced for the size and storage due to its location.

I have lived here for a year and totally loved my experience. First of all this is the ideal location to stay as it is in the city center and close to the university as well. You have plenty of shops in just 2 minute walking distance and the main bus stand for outstation buses is just 5 minute walk from here.
The accommodation manager Rebecca is extremely friendly and kind and makes sure you have a comfortable stay. If you face any maintenance issue they fix it immediately within 24 hours and makes sure the common space is clean and tidy. The heating system works very well and you will always stay warm [this is really important during the winters]. During covid they were kind enough to offer a full refund if we had vacated the room unlike other accommodations. There are 4 secure doors to your room so safety is definitely not an issue. Compared to other student accommodations they are very reasonably priced for the location and facilities. I have had no negative experiences and totally enjoyed my stay at Isca Lofts and recommend it to you.

Now there's no 'rate us 5* for a 'prize" I can be honest about our time here.

Overall the experience is good, but if you're going there be wary of how cash strapped the management appear to be. Not a cheap house though compared to now many rivals. Nice though.
